svg.text: Math Pattern — Number Theory and Arithmetic
• GCD via Euclidean algorithm — gcd(a, b) = gcd(b, a mod b)
• Modular arithmetic — (a + b) mod m = ((a mod m) + (b mod m)) mod m
• Fast exponentiation — O(log n) via repeated squaring
• Sieve of Eratosthenes — primes up to n in O(n log log n)
• Modular inverse — Fermat's little theorem when modulus is prime
• Combinatorics — nCr, factorial precompute, Pascal triangle
• Overflow on int — switch to long, or use modular arithmetic
• Fraction / ratio — track numerator / denominator separately
• Counting modulo 1e9+7 — precompute factorials and inverses
• Geometric — point-line, area, vector cross product
• Random / probability — rejection sampling, reservoir sampling
Identify the operation; the closed form usually exists.
